metadata.notes: agy pre-truncates oversized fields (truncated_fields:["content"], capped ~4KB) so no oversized line ever reaches the parser; the readyâ†’workingâ†’ready arc is fully observable. Driver stubs wait_turn â†’ driver-gap.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

Route: driver-gap (record ports the stubbed wait_turn seam, then drives).

## What the scenario pins

The scenario asserts only the visible state arc `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` with no parser stall causing a stuck `working`; the absence of a stall is INFERRED from the arc completing. Tool calls â‰¤ 0. It is a sanity-check, not a true >2MB cap-stress (the canonical claudecode cell notes 5000 words â‰ˆ 30KB, well under irrlicht's 64MB tailer cap).

## agent_supports: yes

The agy CLI takes a free-form prompt and produces a normal turn; prompting for a single very long sentence is just a long assistant response, which agy handles. No flag needed.

## daemon_capability: full

Key emission finding from the live transcripts: Antigravity itself caps oversized field content BEFORE writing the JSONL line. Every oversized step carries `truncated_fields:["content"]` and the content is hard-capped at ~4KB (observed line at brain/69343e3e.../transcript.jsonl index 3: 5054 bytes, truncated_fields=["content"], content len 4120). So agy NEVER writes a truly oversized line â€” the line the parser sees is always bounded. The JSONLineParser/bufio path therefore never approaches the 2MB scanner limit or the 64MB tailer cap for this scenario; the line is decoded normally and the arc resolves cleanly: USER_INPUTâ†’working, MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE(+tool_calls or not), terminal no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSEâ†’turn_doneâ†’ready. The visible arc is fully observable. (Note: the tailer must still tolerate a multi-KB line, which it does.)

## driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

The interactive recipe needs `wait_turn` to block until the turn completes, but the scaffolded driver-interactive.sh declares DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep" and stubs the wait_turn arm (not_implemented). recipe-lint flags wait_turn as not-elicited â†’ semantic_gap. `record` must port the turn-done poll (SEAM 2 â€” poll the transcript for the terminal no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE) before driving.
caveats: agent_supports/daemon=full is doc+transcript-grounded but PROVISIONAL on a live recording: the very-long-sentence prompt was not driven live here. Because agy pre-truncates, deep >2MB cap-stress is not exercisable from a real agy CLI; a synthetic hand-crafted >2MB line could extend coverage in a follow-up (mirrors the claudecode caveat).
Tokens/cost are not in the JSONL (SQLite protobuf only), so observations assert no cost/tokens for this cell.

SETTLED by live recording (recordings/2026-06-20-13-18-38_irrlichd-0.5.2+76d805a, 2026-06-20): a 600-word-sentence prompt produced a terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE with truncated_fields:["content","thinking"] and content hard-capped at exactly 4120 bytes (largest raw line 8477 bytes) â€” agy pre-truncates before writing, confirming the assessment. The bounded line was parsed without stalling; readyâ†’workingâ†’ready, zero waiting. The earlier 'provisional / not driven live' caveat is now resolved (a true >2MB cap-stress remains non-exercisable from a real agy CLI by design).

sources.note: line index 3: status DONE, truncated_fields:["content"], content capped at 4120 bytes (line 5054 bytes) â€” agy pre-truncates oversized fields before writing
JSONLineParser ParseLine handles bounded lines; turn_done on no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE settles to ready
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; wait_turn arm is not_implemented stub â†’ driver gap

script.text: Write one single continuous sentence of at least 600 words about the history of computing, with no line breaks and no lists. Output only that one sentence.
